message:
feat(unistd): add readpassphrase (OpenBSD passphrase reader) (bd-9fs32)

OpenBSD readpassphrase(prompt, buf, bufsiz, flags) reads a
passphrase from /dev/tty (or stdin/stderr with RPP_STDIN flag)
into a caller-supplied buffer with echo disabled by default.
Companion to the existing getpass shim but supports caller-
provided storage and the documented OpenBSD flag set:
RPP_ECHO_OFF/ON, RPP_REQUIRE_TTY, RPP_FORCELOWER/UPPER,
RPP_SEVENBIT, RPP_STDIN. Used by sshd, sudo, ssh, GPG, and every
passphrase-prompting tool.

Implementation models the existing getpass shim: opens /dev/tty
RDWR (or stdin/stderr per RPP_STDIN), writes the prompt, toggles
ECHO via ioctl(TCGETS/TCSETS) when echo should be off, reads byte-
by-byte until newline / EOF, applies seven-bit / forcelower /
forceupper transformations, restores the terminal, and emits the
suppressed newline. Truncates at bufsiz-1 (drains rest of line on
overflow per OpenBSD).

9 abi integration tests using a temporary stdin pipe (Mutex-
serialized via dup2): NULL buf returns NULL, bufsiz=0 returns
NULL, normal read of "secret123\n" yields "secret123", trailing-
newline stripped, truncation at bufsiz-1, RPP_FORCEUPPER /
RPP_FORCELOWER case conversion, RPP_SEVENBIT high-bit strip,
empty input yields empty string. Clippy-clean, fmt-clean. All
builds via rch.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
